title: Lomariopsis palustris
taxonomy: Lomariopsis palustris is classified as a species of fern in the family Lomariopsidaceae. It belongs to the genus Lomariopsis. This genus is part of the order Polypodiales. The order Polypodiales is a group of ferns that are characterized by having a distinct leaf shape and vascular structure. Lomariopsis palustris is a unique species within this classification due to its specific habitat and growth characteristics.

intro: Lomariopsis palustris, also known as marsh fern, is a type of fern that grows in wetland environments. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as the potential to treat various gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Lomariopsis palustris include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are thought to be responsible for its therapeutic effects.
